frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis
frameworks.one_line: multivariate classification of fMRI signals to decode stimulus features from cortical activity

frameworks.name: sensory recruitment of early visual cortex for working memory
frameworks.one_line: early visual cortex retains stimulus representations during short delay periods

core_question: Does the working-memory decoding effect previously shown for orientation in early visual cortex generalize to stimulus contrast?
key_assumptions: delay-period fMRI responses carry information about maintained stimulus features
multivariate classifiers can read out feature-specific patterns beyond mean response changes
shared representational format permits cross-classification between perceived and remembered stimuli
manipulations: task condition: passive perception vs working-memory delay
classifier train/test direction (perceived-to-remembered and vice versa)
key_findings: fMRI patterns in human visual cortex decoded the contrast of a perceived stimulus after accounting for mean response changes
fMRI responses also supported decoding of contrast when the stimulus had to be remembered
classification generalized from perceived to remembered stimuli and vice versa
interpretations: working-memory traces in early visual cortex extend beyond orientation to other visual features such as contrast
perception and short-term maintenance share a highly consistent representational pattern in early visual cortex
